COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE
SALE AND PURCHASE AGREEMENT
Emirate of Dubai I United Arab Emirates
(In accordance with Dubai Law No. 7 of 2006, Real Estate Regulatory. Agency, [RERA] Regulations, and Dubai Land
Department [DLD] Requirements)
Agreement Date
Agreement Reference No.
DLD Title Deed No.
01 January 2026
DXB-SPA-2026-001504
DLD-2026-458921
THIS COMMERCIAL SALE AND PURCHASE AGREEMENT (hereinafter referred to as the
"Agreement") is entered into on the date specified above, BY AND BETWEEN the Parties described
herein, with reference to the following recitals:
WHEREAS, the Seller is the registered and lawful owner of the commercial property described herein,
holding valid Title Deed issued by the Dubai Land Department;
WHEREAS, the Buyer desires to purchase the said Property from the Seller, and the Seller desires to
sell the said Property to the Buyer, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement;
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants, representations, warranties, and
agreements herein contained, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and
sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:
SECTION 1 - PARTIES TO THE AGREEMENT

SECTION 4 - PAYMENT SCHEDULE AND METHOD

SECTION 5 - TRANSFER OF OWNERSHIP AND DLD REGISTRATION
5.1. TRANSFER DATE
The parties agree to complete the transfer of ownership on or before 15 February 2026 (the
"Transfer Date"). The venue for transfer shall be a DLD-approved Trustee Office in Dubai, as
mutually agreed by the parties.
5.2. DLD REGISTRATION CONDITIONS
Ownership shall transfer only upon: (a) Full payment of the purchase price and all applicable DLD
fees; (b) Submission of all required KYC, corporate authorization documents, No Objection
Certificate (NOC) from the developer/building management, and other documents required by the
Dubai Land Department; (c) Successful registration of the transfer in the DLD system and issuance
Confidential I Governed by UAE Laws & Dubai Land Department Regulations I Page 3 of9



COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE: SALE AND PURCHASE. AGREEMENT I DUBAI, UAE
of a new Title Deed in the Buyer's name; (d) Clearance of any outstanding service charges and
utility bills by the Seller.
5.3. DEVELOPER NOC
The Seller shall obtain and provide to the Buyer, at Seller's cost, a No Objection Certificate (NOC)
from the relevant building developer/owners association, confirming that the Property is free from
any dues and the developer has no objection to the sale and transfer. The NOC shall be obtained
no later than 7 (seven) working days prior to the Transfer Date.
5.4. OQOOD I INTERIM REGISTRATION
If the Property is subject to an existing off-plan registration, the Seller shall ensure proper
novation/transfer of the Oqood record and comply with DLD off-plan transfer procedures before
issuance of a final Title Deed to the Buyer.
SECTION 6 - PROPERTY CONDITION AND INSPECTION
6.1. AS-IS SALE
The Buyer acknowledges that it has inspected the Property, including all its fixtures, fittings, plant,
equipment, and installations, and is satisfied with the condition of the Property. The Buyer agrees to
purchase the Property on an "as-is where-is" basis, subject to normal wear and tear.
6.2. SNAGGING
The Seller shall be responsible for rectifying any major structural defects, electrical faults, or
plumbing issues identified in a pre-transfer inspection report, provided such defects are reported to
the Seller in writing at least 7 days before the Transfer Date. Minor cosmetic issues shall not
constitute grounds for delaying the transfer.
6.3. INVENTORY
An agreed inventory list of fixtures and fittings included in the sale is annexed to this Agreement as
Annexure I. Items listed therein shall be handed over to the Buyer on the Transfer Date.
SECTION 7 - VACANT POSSESSION AND HANDOVER
7.1. VACANT POSSESSION
The Seller warrants that the Property shall be delivered with full vacant possession on the Transfer
Date. The Property is currently unoccupied and not subject to any tenancy, license, or third-party
occupation rights.
7.2. EXISTING TENANCY (IF APPLICABLE)
If the Property is subject to an existing tenancy agreement, the Seller shall provide full disclosure in
Annexure II, including a copy of the tenancy contract registered with Ejari, current rent amount,
lease expiry date, and RERA applicable notice periods. The Buyer acknowledges and accepts the
disclosed tenancy as a condition of sale.
7.3. HANDOVER PROCESS
Confidential I Governed by UAE Laws & Dubai Land Department Regulations I Page 4 of 9



COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE: SALE AND PURCHASE. AGREEMENT I DUBAI, UAE
On the Transfer Date, the Seller shall hand over to the Buyer: (a) All sets of keys, access cards,
and fobs; (b) Copies of all relevant Property documents including the original Title Deed; (C) All fit-
out permits, completion certificates, and NOCS received from authorities; (d) DEWA connection
details and existing account information.
SECTION 8 - REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES OF SELLER
The Seller represents and warrants to the Buyer that, as of the date of this Agreement and as of the
Transfer Date:
The Seller is the sole legal and beneficial owner of the Property and has full right, title, and
authority to sell and transfer the Property to the Buyer.
The Property is free from all encumbrances, mortgages, charges, liens, attachments, disputes,
litigation, or third-party claims, except as expressly disclosed in this Agreement.
There are no pending or threatened legal proceedings before any court, arbitration panel, or
regulatory authority relating to the Property.
All service charges, maintenance fees, utility bills (DEWA), property taxes (if applicable), and
community fees have been paid in full up to the Transfer Date.
The Property has been constructed and maintained in compliance with all applicable UAE laws,
building codes, and municipal regulations.
No unauthorized alterations, additions, or modifications have been made to the Property without
prior approval from the relevant authorities.
The Seller has obtained all necessary corporate approvals and authorizations to execute this
Agreement and complete the sale.
The Seller is not aware of any material fact or circumstance that would adversely affect the
value, use, or marketability of the Property that has not been disclosed to the Buyer.
SECTION 9 - REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES AND OBLIGATIONS OF BUYER
The Buyer represents, warrants and covenants as follows:
The Buyer has full legal capacity and corporate authority to enter into this Agreement and to
purchase the Property.
The Buyer shall pay the Purchase Price strictly in accordance with the payment schedule set
out in Section 4.
The Buyer shall provide all KYC documents (including Trade License, Memorandum of
Association, Board Resolution authorizing the purchase, authorized signatory passport/Emirates
ID), as required by the DLD, Trustee Office, and applicable AML/CFT laws.
The Buyer shall attend the DLD Trustee Office appointment on the Transfer Date, or provide a
duly notarized Power of Attorney for an authorized representative to attend on its behalf.
The Buyer shall bear and pay all registration, transfer, and administrative fees payable to the
DLD and Trustee Office, as specified in Section 10 herein.
The Buyer shall conduct its own independent due diligence on the Property, including title
verification, encumbrance search at the DLD, physical inspection, and legal review, and the
Seller shall cooperate in facilitating such due diligence.
The source of funds used by the Buyer for the purchase of the Property is lawful and compliant
with the UAE AMLICFT Federal Law No. 20 of 2018.
Confidential I Governed by UAE Laws & Dubai Land Department Regulations I Page 5 of9



COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE: SALE AND PURCHASE. AGREEMENT I DUBAI, UAE

SECTION 11 = RERA COMPLIANCE AND DLD FORM F
11.1. RERA FORM F
The parties acknowledge that this Agreement shall be supplemented by a duly executed RERA
Form F (Memorandum of Understanding), generated through the DLD-approved Dubai REST App
or Trakheesi system by the licensed RERA broker. In the event of any conflict between this
Agreement and the RERA Form F, the provisions of the RERA Form F shall prevail to the extent of
regulatory requirements.
11.2. RERA BROKER REGISTRATION
The transaction has been facilitated by licensed RERA Brokers holding valid Broker Registration
Numbers (BRN). Any advertising of this Property has been or will be conducted under a valid
Trakheesi permit issued by DLD. Unauthorized advertising is a violation of RERA Law.
11.3. AMLICFT COMPLIANCE
All parties confirm compliance with UAE Federal Law No. 20 of 2018 on Anti-Money Laundering
and Combating the Financing of Terrorism. The Seller and Buyer shall complete all KYCIAML
Confidential I Governed by UAE Laws & Dubai Land Department Regulations I Page 6 of9



COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE: SALE AND PURCHASE. AGREEMENT I DUBAI, UAE
documentation required by the Trustee Office, Real Estate Registration Trustee, and any financing
institution involved in the transaction.
SECTION 12 - DEFAULT AND REMEDIES
12.1. DEFAULT BY BUYER
If the Buyer fails to pay the Purchase Price (or any installment thereof) on the due date, or fails to
appear for the DLD transfer appointment without lawful reason, the Seller shall have the right to: (a)
Retain the Security Deposit as liquidated damages; (b) Rescind this Agreement upon 14 days'
written notice to the Buyer; (c) Re-list and sell the Property to any third party; (d) Pursue any
additional remedies available under Dubai law and regulations.
12.2. DEFAULT BY SELLER
If the Seller fails to transfer clear title or vacate the Property without lawful reason, or breaches any
material warranty herein, the Buyer shall have the right to: (a) Demand specific performance of this
Agreement through the competent courts of Dubai; (b) Recover the Security Deposit plus interest at
EIBOR + 2% per annum from the Transfer Date; (c) Claim reasonable costs, losses, and expenses
incurred as a result of the Seller's breach; (d) Seek any additional remedies available under UAE
and Dubai law.
12.3. MUTUAL TERMINATION
Both parties may mutually agree in writing to terminate this Agreement at any time before the
Transfer Date, in which case the terms of such termination, including refund of the deposit, shall be
as mutually agreed in the termination agreement.
SECTION 13 - FORCE MAJEURE
Neither party shall be held liable or in breach of its obligations under this Agreement to the extent that
performance is prevented or delayed by any force majeure event, including but not limited to: act of
God; natural disaster; epidemic or pandemic; war or armed conflict; government-imposed sanctions or
restrictions; regulatory authority actions; or any event beyond the reasonable control of the affected
party. The affected party shall promptly notify the other party in writing of the occurrence of such event.
If a force majeure event persists for more than 60 (sixty) calendar days, either party may terminate this
Agreement by giving 14 days' written notice, and the Security Deposit shall be refunded to the Buyer
without any deduction.
SECTION 14 - DISPUTE RESOLUTION AND GOVERNING LAW
14.1. GOVERNING LAW
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the United Arab
Emirates, as applicable in the Emirate of Dubai, including but not limited to: Dubai Law No. 7 of
2006 (Real Property Registration Law); Dubai Law No. 13 of 2008 (Interim Real Property Register);
UAE Federal Law No. 5 of 1985 (Civil Transactions Law); and RERA Regulations and DLD
directives as amended from time to time.
14.2. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Confidential I Governed by UAE Laws & Dubai Land Department Regulations I Page 7 of9



COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE: SALE AND PURCHASE. AGREEMENT I DUBAI, UAE
The parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute arising from or in connection with this
Agreement amicably through good-faith negotiations within 21 (twenty-one) days of written notice of
the dispute. If the dispute cannot be resolved amicably, it shall be referred to the Dubai Land
Department's Real Estate Dispute Resolution Centre (RDRC) or the competent courts of Dubai, as
applicable, which shall have exclusive jurisdiction.
14.3. LANGUAGE
This Agreement is executed in the English language. In the event it is translated into Arabic, the
Arabic version shall prevail for the purpose of DLD registration. Both parties confirm their
understanding of all terms herein.
SECTION 15 = GENERAL PROVISIONS
15.1. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Agreement (together with all Annexures hereto) constitutes the entire agreement between the
parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations,
representations, statements, and agreements, whether oral or written.
15.2. AMENDMENTS
No amendment, modification, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be valid unless
made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both parties.
15.3. SEVERABILITY
If any provision of this Agreement is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of
competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.
15.4. NOTICES
All notices under this Agreement shall be made in writing and delivered by registered mail, courier,
or email with read receipt to the addresses stated in Section 1. Notice by email shall be effective
upon receipt of read confirmation.
15.5. ANNEXURES
The following Annexures are attached hereto and form an integral part of this Agreement: Annexure
I-I Inventory List of Fixtures & Fittings; Annexure II - Existing Tenancy Details (if applicable);
Annexure III I- Copy of Title Deed; Annexure IV - Building Floor Plan / Unit Layout; Annexure V-
Developer NOC (to be provided before Transfer Date).
SECTION 16 = EXECUTION AND SIGNATURES
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date first written above, in
the presence of the witnesses named below.
FOR AND ON BEHALF OF - SELLER
FOR AND ON BEHALF OF - BUYER
Al Noor Properties LLC
Global Trade Investments FZCO
